creator: galen.sampson@gmail.com
text:
Tom Russo wrote:
> On Wed, Feb 11, 2009 at 09:05:19PM -0700, Galen Sampson <galen.sampson@gmail.com> wrote:
>   
>> Tom Russo wrote:
>>     
>>> I was bitten by this today when I foolishly did a mass update on a machine
>>> that requires NVIDIA 96xx drivers, only to find that now I can't start the
>>> X server with the accelerated GL anymore.
>>>
>>> Nosing around on the NVIDIA FreeBSD forums
>>> (http://www.nvnews.net/vbulletin/showthread.php?s=0d3c319ae84965e2c3ab00850c3e6e0b&t=127015)
>>> I find that on 27 January 2009 they released a beta 96.43.10 driver for FreeBSD.
>>>
>>>
>>>   
>>>       
>> Thank you for the research.  I will try it out.
>>     
>
> Did it work?
>
> It would be nice if the port could be updated so that this driver
> could be used again.  
>
>
>
>   
Yes.  I tried it and it worked for me.  I can definately use Xorg 
version 1.5.3 from ports with this beta nvidia driver and an Nvidia 
Ti4200.  It is a little strange in that it loads the linux.ko module.  I 
have not had experienced any crashes or otherwise, but I haven't really 
stressed the system very hard.

Regards,
Galen
